title: Add JSON version of the machine list
body:
Adds `--format=json` machine-readable output, to `podman machine list`.

See https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/11691#issuecomment-925032210

comments.body: LGTM once comment from @Luap99 is addressed
LGTM once @Luap99 's tweak is tweaked.  Thx @afbjorklund !
@afbjorklund try with adding quotes: `podman machine ls --format "{{.[TAB]"`. Sometimes it only works with the quotes around it, I don't know why.

It works when you add the quotes on both sides, without quotes or only opening quotes then it fails.

You also **must** go back one character, or it will just remove the quotes - and then it starts all over...

```
./bin/podman machine list --format 
./bin/podman machine list --format json
```

```
./bin/podman machine list --format {{.
./bin/podman machine list --format {{.
```

```
./bin/podman machine list --format "{{."
./bin/podman machine list --format {{.
```

```
./bin/podman machine list --format "{{.
./bin/podman machine list --format "{{.
```

```
./bin/podman machine list --format "{{."
{{.CPUs}}      {{.Default}}   {{.LastUp}}    {{.Name}}      {{.VMType}}    
{{.Created}}   {{.DiskSize}}  {{.Memory}}    {{.Running}}
```
Can you try `source <(bin/podman completion bash)`. I think it should work without quotes if you use the latest shell script.

> Can you try `source <(bin/podman completion bash)`. I think it should work without quotes if you use the latest shell script.

I don't even get any debug entry, so for some reason bash is not using the completion when it looks like `{{.` and not `"{{."` ?
